rationale: The cited paper is a methodological guide on probing interactions in moderated regression (spotlight/floodlight, Johnson-Neyman), and the citing paper reports multiple experiments with moderation/boundary-condition tests (e.g., product positioning salience and involvement effects). Given this clear methodological fit and a direct DOI-matched reference (10.1509/jmr.12.0420), the citation could be supporting core analysis choices rather than being a purely incidental mention.
